raw_themes.name: Zero-schema MCP from CLI introspection
raw_themes.short_desc: Auto-generate MCP tool definitions by introspecting existing Click CLI metadata; eliminates manual schema duplication. ~200 LOC.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Zero manual schema definitions -- they introspect Click CLI metadata and generate MCP tools dynamically. ~200 lines. Cleanest MCP implementation in the T29 corpus.

raw_themes.name: Hook execution model with runtime semantics
raw_themes.short_desc: Naming-encoded hook semantics: event family, 2-digit ordering, fg/bg distinction, SIGTERM finalization, JSONL stdout contract.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: naming convention (on*{EventFamily}\_\_{order}*{name}[.bg].{ext}), 2-digit ordering, foreground/background with SIGTERM finalization, JSONL stdout contract.

raw_themes.name: CLAUDE.md as philosophy document
raw_themes.short_desc: 498-line CLAUDE.md encodes philosophy (grep-friendly naming, minimize-unique-names, NO MOCKS) beyond pure setup instructions.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The CLAUDE.md is quietly one of the best developer onboarding documents in the Python open-source ecosystem. It encodes philosophy, not just setup.

raw_themes.name: Coverage-as-dead-code detector
raw_themes.short_desc: Use pytest-cov JSON + jq to find 0% coverage files as dead-code candidates; passive collection during dev.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Coverage-as-dead-code-detector (JSON+jq for 0% files) relevant to T21 orphan detection.

raw_themes.name: NO MOCKS testing standard
raw_themes.short_desc: Real SQLite, real subprocess — no artificial seams. Strict equality assertions (== not >=) force regressions to surface.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The NO MOCKS testing standard forces the codebase to remain testable without artificial seams.

raw_themes.name: Governance gap behind healthy community signal
raw_themes.short_desc: 27K stars, 0/29 changesets reviewed. 100% community health masks solo-maintainer architecture and permissive security defaults.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 27K stars, 0/29 changesets reviewed. If JASON-OS scales, enforce review from day one.

raw_candidates.name: MCP auto-discovery from CLI metadata
raw_candidates.description: Zero-schema MCP server that introspects Click CLI commands and auto-generates MCP tool definitions. ~200 lines. Uses CliRunner for execution. Eliminates manual schema duplication.

raw_candidates.name: Hook execution model with ordering and bg/fg lifecycle
raw_candidates.description: Production-grade hook system: event-family naming convention, 2-digit ordering prefix, foreground/background distinction, SIGTERM finalization for background hooks, JSONL stdout contract.

raw_candidates.name: CLAUDE.md as structured developer philosophy document
raw_candidates.description: 498-line CLAUDE.md encoding not just setup instructions but development philosophy: grep-friendly naming, minimize-unique-names, NO MOCKS testing standard, coverage-as-dead-code methodology.

raw_candidates.name: Coverage-as-dead-code-detector methodology
raw_candidates.description: Using pytest-cov JSON output + jq pipelines to find 0% coverage files as dead code candidates. Passive coverage collection during dev server usage for broader signal.

raw_candidates.name: Claude Code in CI (claude.yml workflow)
raw_candidates.description: Claude Code running as GitHub Actions workflow in CI pipeline. Pattern for AI-assisted development automation.

raw_candidates.name: Strict assertion testing convention
raw_candidates.description: Use exact counts (==) not loose bounds (>=) in test assertions. Forces tests to catch regressions rather than just pass.

raw_candidates.name: SOLO_MAINTAINER_GOVERNANCE
raw_candidates.description: 27K stars but 0/29 changesets with code review. Architectural decisions in prose docs, not enforced contracts. 100% community health score masks governance gap.

raw_candidates.name: PERMISSIVE_DEFAULTS_FOR_SELF_HOSTED
raw_candidates.description: ALLOWED_HOSTS='*', SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=False, no HTTPS enforcement. Security-by-configuration rather than security-by-default.

raw_candidates.name: CONFIGURED_NOT_ENFORCED_TYPING
raw_candidates.description: Pyright configured in pyproject.toml but 48% type coverage with heavy Any usage. Type checker as CI decoration rather than enforcement gate.

archivebox-archivebox.relevance_to_home: Direct hit on JASON-OS Domain 02a: ArchiveBox's zero-schema MCP auto-discovery from Click CLI is the cleanest MCP implementation in the corpus and teaches SoNash a runtime hook model to complement its governance. The CLAUDE.md philosophy and coverage-as-dead-code methodology are near-drop-in patterns.

raw_themes.name: Curated-list celebrity stagnation
raw_themes.short_desc: 486K stars, 462 open issues, 1 commit in 90 days — growth outpaced the curator's attention. Cautionary model for the SoNash skill index.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 486K stars, 462 open issues, 1 commit in 90 days. It grew through community enthusiasm, hit a maintenance bottleneck, and is now in celebrity stagnation.

raw_themes.name: Missing retirement process as catalog failure mode
raw_themes.short_desc: Curated lists without retirement/archival process accumulate until unmaintainable. Skill-audit is defensive, but a retirement workflow is missing.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: your skill index is becoming a curated list, and curated lists have a known failure mode.

raw_themes.name: Feynman 'build to understand' framing
raw_themes.short_desc: Build-your-own-X teaches via from-scratch implementation. Reframes JASON-OS Domain 01 (Internal Archaeology) from inventory to narrative.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: the fastest way to truly learn a technology is to build it yourself.

raw_themes.name: Single-file-everything anti-pattern
raw_themes.short_desc: 390 links in one README.md works at small scale, unmaintainable at large scale. Watch EXTRACTIONS.md / MEMORY.md for same impulse.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: This repo puts 390 links in one README.md. It works at small scale and becomes unmaintainable at large scale.

raw_themes.name: Inline-license-without-LICENSE-file trap
raw_themes.short_desc: CC0 in README footer but no LICENSE file means GitHub API reports 'no license'; automated tools misflag.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The README claims CC0 but there's no LICENSE file. GitHub's API reports 'no license.' This means automated tools (including yours) will flag it incorrectly.

raw_candidates.name: Skill retirement process design
raw_candidates.description: Case study in what happens when a curated collection grows without a retirement/archival process. 486K stars, 462 open issues, 1 commit/90d. Apply this lesson to JASON-OS skill lifecycle.

raw_candidates.name: 'Build from scratch' framing for Domain 01
raw_candidates.description: Feynman principle applied to system understanding. Structure JASON-OS Internal Archaeology as 'how each system was built' narratives rather than 'what exists' inventories.

raw_candidates.name: Celebrity stagnation lifecycle
raw_candidates.description: Full lifecycle: community enthusiasm -> commercial acquisition -> maintenance bottleneck -> dormancy. Cautionary case study for any future community distribution model.

raw_candidates.name: 31-Category Tutorial Taxonomy
raw_candidates.description: 31 technology categories organized for 'build from scratch' learning. Useful as a reference for organizing skill categories or learning paths.

raw_candidates.name: Build Your Own React (Pomber)
raw_candidates.description: Canonical React-from-scratch tutorial: fiber architecture, reconciliation, hooks. Improves component design and re-render diagnosis for React 19 / SoNash work.

raw_candidates.name: Write Yourself a Git
raw_candidates.description: Python from-scratch Git implementation. Understanding the object model helps design better hook infrastructure and worktree tooling.

raw_candidates.name: Regex Matching Can Be Simple and Fast
raw_candidates.description: NFA/DFA theory from Russ Cox. Helps write more efficient regex in check-pattern-compliance.js and avoid the two-strikes regex anti-pattern.

raw_candidates.name: Single-file-everything pattern
raw_candidates.description: 390 links in one README.md. Works at small scale, unmaintainable at large scale. Watch for same impulse in EXTRACTIONS.md, MEMORY.md, SKILL_INDEX.md.

raw_candidates.name: Inline license without LICENSE file
raw_candidates.description: CC0 in README footer but no LICENSE file. GitHub API reports 'no license'. Automated tools can't detect it. Always include a machine-readable LICENSE file.

codecrafters-io-build-your-own-x.relevance_to_home: Not a code source but a canonical case study: SoNash has 72+ skills and JASON-OS will accumulate more. This source's celebrity-stagnation trajectory is the exact failure mode skill-audit can't prevent without a retirement process. Also surfaces single-file-everything anti-pattern relevant to EXTRACTIONS.md / MEMORY.md.

raw_themes.name: Agent-native CLI wrapping methodology (HARNESS.md)
raw_themes.short_desc: 7-phase SOP for wrapping arbitrary GUI apps as agent-invokable CLIs. Language-agnostic in principle, Python in implementation.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The HARNESS.md is the intellectual heart. It's a 7-phase SOP that takes you from "I have a GUI app" to "agents can operate it."

raw_themes.name: Registry + Hub + meta-skill distribution
raw_themes.short_desc: registry.json (35 entries) served via static CLI-Hub; meta-skill makes catalog agent-discoverable and pip-installable without human intervention.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The registry pattern (35 CLIs in `registry.json`, served via CLI-Hub static site) solves the discovery problem.

raw_themes.name: Adversarial-input security posture for AI agents
raw_themes.short_desc: SECURITY.md explicitly names the threat: agents autonomously constructing commands from untrusted input. Codec allowlists + never-shell=True.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: SECURITY.md names the actual threat: "an AI agent may autonomously construct and execute commands based on untrusted input."

raw_themes.name: Session file-locking for concurrent JSON ops
raw_themes.short_desc: _locked_save_json: open r+, fcntl.flock exclusive, truncate+write inside lock. Graceful Windows degradation.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: `_locked_save_json`: open `"r+"` (no truncation), `fcntl.flock` exclusive, then truncate+write inside the lock.

raw_themes.name: SKILL.md as command-catalog vs workflow-definition
raw_themes.short_desc: Two distinct SKILL.md formats: flat command reference (CLI-Anything, ~183 LOC) vs multi-phase workflow (SoNash, ~350 LOC). Different abstraction levels.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Their SKILL.md (mermaid: 183 lines, drawio: 213 lines) is: YAML frontmatter + Installation + Basic Commands + Command Groups + Examples. Your SKILL.md (repo-analysis: ~350 lines) is: YAML frontmatter + Critical Rules + When to Use + Process Overview + Phase definitions + Guard Rails.

raw_themes.name: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json format reference
raw_themes.short_desc: First public Claude Code plugin format in the wild: marketplace.json + 5 slash command definitions in commands/.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: `marketplace.json` + 5 slash command definitions in `commands/` (cli-anything.md, list.md, refine.md, test.md, validate.md). First public Claude Code plugin reference.

raw_themes.name: Growth-outpacing-quality-gates
raw_themes.short_desc: 28K stars in 29 days, 35 harnesses, zero CI test enforcement. Opposite lesson from build-your-own-x stagnation — same risk, compressed timeline.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 28K stars in 29 days with 30+ contributors and no automated quality enforcement is a recipe for the same trajectory as build-your-own-x — just faster.

raw_candidates.name: HARNESS.md 7-Phase SOP
raw_candidates.description: Systematic methodology for wrapping GUI apps as agent-native CLIs. 7 phases: codebase analysis -> CLI architecture -> implementation -> backend integration -> export -> session -> REPL + SKILL.md generation.

hkuds-cli-anything.relevance_to_home: Highest-density source for JASON-OS Domain 02a in the slice: HARNESS.md is a transferable methodology, the registry/hub is a distribution model SoNash lacks, and .claude-plugin/marketplace.json is the first concrete reference for Claude Code plugin distribution. Build-vs-integrate decision is a concrete architecture fork.

raw_themes.name: Document parsing as orchestration problem
raw_themes.short_desc: Three backends (pipeline/VLM/vLLM) + router + string-dispatched factory with deferred imports. Match inference runtime to model size.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: MinerU understands that **document parsing is an orchestration problem, not a model problem**.

raw_themes.name: Structured output as product surface
raw_themes.short_desc: output_files.md: typed fields, 4-level block hierarchy, enumerated block types, bbox convention, explicit breaking-change callouts. Schema as first-class docs.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Most ML projects treat their output schema as an implementation detail. MinerU treats it as a product surface.

raw_themes.name: Async task state machine with TTL cleanup
raw_themes.short_desc: In-memory task dict, 4 states (pending/processing/completed/failed), asyncio.Queue work channel, semaphore concurrency, background TTL sweep, FS-backed artifacts.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: MinerU's `POST /tasks` endpoint is exactly this pattern at a smaller scale. It's an in-memory dict of task state, an `asyncio.Queue` as the work channel, a semaphore capping concurrency, four states (`pending/processing/completed/failed`)

raw_themes.name: Environment variable catalog as single document
raw_themes.short_desc: Prose catalog of 15+ MINERU_* env vars with default/effect/scoping. Discipline beats content — SoNash has scattered SKIP_*/GSD_*/CAS_* with no central reference.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: `docs/en/usage/cli_tools.md` has a prose catalog of 15+ `MINERU_*` env vars, each with default, effect, scoping rules, and cross-references.

raw_themes.name: Backend decision matrix before install
raw_themes.short_desc: 5-backend x 8-constraint matrix in quick_start/index.md lets user pick before installing. Pattern for /analyze routing across source types/depths.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 5-column × 8-row decision matrix mapping backend to operational constraints (accuracy, OS support, CPU/GPU support, min VRAM, RAM, disk, Python version). User picks before installing.

raw_themes.name: Coarse-to-fine two-stage parsing
raw_themes.short_desc: MinerU 2.5: stage 1 downsampled global layout, stage 2 native-resolution local crops. Analogue for SoNash /repo-analysis depth tiers.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: MinerU 2.5 uses a two-stage strategy that decouples global layout analysis (stage 1, downsampled images, structural elements identified cheaply) from local content recognition (stage 2, native-resolution crops, fine detail preserved).

raw_themes.name: HTTP service without default hardening
raw_themes.short_desc: mineru-api has no auth, no rate limit, no upload cap, no CORS/TrustedHost, FastAPI docs enabled. Anti-pattern SoNash security-auditor exists to prevent.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: mineru-api has no authentication — not even an API key — no rate limiting, no upload size cap, no CORS middleware, no TrustedHost middleware, and FastAPI docs enabled by default.

raw_themes.name: One-fuzzy-string-match test gated by 0.2% coverage
raw_themes.short_desc: 58K-star codebase held together by a single e2e test. Silent retry workflow converts flake to green. SoNash's test discipline is the correct counter-model.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: A 58k-star codebase is held together by one fuzzy string match gated by a 0.2% coverage floor.

raw_candidates.name: Async task state machine with TTL cleanup
raw_candidates.description: In-memory task dict with four states (pending/processing/completed/failed), asyncio.Queue work channel, asyncio.Semaphore concurrency cap, background sweep for TTL cleanup, FS-backed output artifacts keyed by task ID. Ephemeral state. Answers T28 Gap Agent A Q5 (progressive extraction).

raw_candidates.name: Scored multi-worker load balancer
raw_candidates.description: Formula: score = (queued + processing + pending_assignments) / max_concurrent. Shuffle-then-stable-sort for jitter. Optimistic pending_assignments increment prevents thundering herd.

raw_candidates.name: Sliding window for long-input memory bounding
raw_candidates.description: get_processing_window_size(default=64) applied identically across all three backends. Pages loaded, inferred, freed in window-sized batches. Peak memory strictly bounded.

raw_candidates.name: Wrap upstream CLI instead of reimplementing
raw_candidates.description: vllm_server.py is ~70 lines that intercept sys.argv for existing vllm CLI, inject per-device args and custom logits processor, then hand off. Don't fork — wrap with project-specific defaults.

raw_candidates.name: Structured output contract as first-class document
raw_candidates.description: Human-readable specification of middle.json IR: typed fields, 4-level block hierarchy, enumerated block types, bbox convention, explicit breaking-change callouts between pipeline and VLM backends.

raw_candidates.name: Environment variable catalog as single document
raw_candidates.description: Prose catalog of 15+ MINERU_* env vars with default, effect, scoping rules. Discipline is what matters, not the content. Template for docs/agent_docs/ENV_VARS.md.

raw_candidates.name: Backend decision matrix for multi-mode tools
raw_candidates.description: 5-column x 8-row decision matrix mapping backend to operational constraints (accuracy, OS, CPU/GPU, VRAM, RAM, disk, Python). User picks before installing. Pattern for /analyze routing docs.

raw_candidates.name: Coarse-to-fine two-stage parsing (MinerU 2.5 paper)
raw_candidates.description: Two-stage strategy: stage 1 downsampled global layout, stage 2 native-resolution local crops. Coarse-to-fine pattern generalizes to SoNash depth tiers.

raw_candidates.name: POST /tasks API documentation
raw_candidates.description: Async task API four-state machine + TTL cleanup + semaphore gating documented in prose. Read alongside fast_api.py for progressive extraction shape.

raw_candidates.name: output_files.md schema reference
raw_candidates.description: Exemplary structured-output spec page. Template for SoNash's ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md.

raw_candidates.name: Single-test fuzzy-match e2e with low coverage floor
raw_candidates.description: A 58k-star codebase is held together by one fuzzy string match gated by a 0.2% coverage floor. Shows up in MinerU's 160-issue backlog as parsing regressions.

raw_candidates.name: Unarmored HTTP service with insecure defaults
raw_candidates.description: mineru-api ships with no auth, no rate limit, no upload size cap, no CORS, no TrustedHost, FastAPI docs enabled. Docs encourage --host 0.0.0.0 for multi-GPU.

raw_candidates.name: Silent retry workflow converts flake into green
raw_candidates.description: Automatic retry of failed CI runs up to 3x without surfacing the flake rate. Opposite of silent-failure-hunter discipline.

raw_candidates.name: Broken i18n parity (Chinese-only chip guides)
raw_candidates.description: 13 Chinese-only hardware guides with no English equivalents while README claims '10+ chip support'. Credibility gap.

raw_candidates.name: No PR test gating (push-only workflows)
raw_candidates.description: cli.yml runs tests only on pushes to master/dev, never on pull requests. Contributors can't see test results before merge. SoNash PR gating is the correct model.

raw_candidates.name: README integration claim unbacked by first-party code
raw_candidates.description: README lists 'MCP Server — Cursor · Claude Desktop · Windsurf' in integration matrix. Reality: opendatalab/mineru-mcp 404s. Community implementations only. Misleading.

raw_candidates.name: Unpinned GitHub Action reference
raw_candidates.description: mkdocs.yml uses mhausenblas/mkdocs-deploy-gh-pages@master. Inconsistent with repo's other pinned action (@v2.6.1). Compromise of upstream = arbitrary doc-site publishing.

raw_candidates.name: Floor-only version constraint on security-sensitive library
raw_candidates.description: pillow>=11.0.0 with no upper bound. CVE-2026-25990 affects 10.3.0 to <12.1.1. Resolver-picked version determines exposure. Fix: ranged constraint.

MinerU.relevance_to_home: Two T1 lifts (output-contract page, env-vars catalog) are pure writing-discipline — half-day each and directly answer existing SoNash doc-drift tensions in CONVENTIONS.md §13 and §17. The async task state-machine shape informs T28 Gap Agent A Q5 (progressive extraction) even though the crawl4ai port is the recommended lift. 9 anti-patterns confirm or extend SoNash's existing §5 guardrails.

raw_themes.name: Confidence tagging (EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS)
raw_themes.short_desc: Every edge tagged with provenance. Rare honesty about what was observed vs guessed. Applicable to all SoNash analysis findings and /recall results.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Every edge in the graph is tagged EXTRACTED, INFERRED, or AMBIGUOUS — you always know whether the tool found a relationship or guessed it. That honesty is rare.

raw_themes.name: Dual-pass extraction (AST deterministic + LLM semantic)
raw_themes.short_desc: Pass 1 tree-sitter parses 20 languages, zero LLM cost. Pass 2 Claude subagents extract semantic relationships. Code-only corpuses are free.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The first pass is deterministic: tree-sitter parses ASTs for 20 languages, extracting classes, functions, imports, and call graphs with zero LLM cost. The second pass uses Claude subagents in parallel to extract semantic relationships

raw_themes.name: 10-step skill orchestration pipeline
raw_themes.short_desc: Most structured multi-step skill example in corpus: install+detect inline, parallel subagent dispatch with cache checking, 6 pipeline stages, platform variants.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The 10-step pipeline with parallel agent dispatch is the most mature example I've seen of a complex multi-step skill.

raw_themes.name: Stateless token-budgeted MCP server
raw_themes.short_desc: 320-line MCP stdio server loads graph once, exposes 7 tools with _subgraph_to_text token budgeting. Simpler than SoNash's MCP memory server.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: A 320-line MCP server that loads a graph once and exposes 7 tools. Clean, stateless, no network listener.

raw_themes.name: Threat-vector-organized security model
raw_themes.short_desc: SECURITY.md organized by attack vector with mitigation + 'what graphify does NOT do' sections. More readable than checklist-based formats.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Their threat model is organized by attack vector, not by module. Each vector has a mitigation and a "what graphify does NOT do" section.

raw_themes.name: LanguageConfig dataclass + generic walker
raw_themes.short_desc: One config per language with type-specific resolution. Generic walker extracts 20 languages with zero duplication. Pattern for SoNash analysis handlers.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: One config object per language with type-specific node resolution, call detection, and import handling. A generic walker uses the config to extract all 20 languages with zero code duplication.

raw_themes.name: ARCHITECTURE.md single-page discipline
raw_themes.short_desc: One page: pipeline + module table + schema + confidence labels + how-to-extend + security + testing. No bloat.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: A single page covering pipeline, module table, extraction schema, confidence labels, how to add a language, security model, and test instructions. No bloat.

raw_candidates.name: Skill orchestration pipeline (10-step, parallel agents)
raw_candidates.description: 10-step pipeline with parallel agent dispatch, cache checking, chunk sizing. Most structured multi-step skill example found. Directly comparable to SoNash skill architecture.

raw_candidates.name: Confidence tagging taxonomy (EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS)
raw_candidates.description: Three-tier confidence taxonomy for graph edges. EXTRACTED = directly observed, INFERRED = model-reasoned, AMBIGUOUS = uncertain. Applicable to analysis findings and recall results.

raw_candidates.name: MCP server pattern (stateless, token-budgeted)
raw_candidates.description: 320-line MCP stdio server. Load graph once, expose 7 tools. Token budget on subgraph rendering. No network listener.

raw_candidates.name: LanguageConfig dataclass (generic walker + per-type config)
raw_candidates.description: One config object per language with type-specific node resolution, call detection, import handling. Generic walker uses config to extract 20 languages with zero duplication.

raw_candidates.name: Threat-vector security model format
raw_candidates.description: Security model organized by attack vector with mitigations table and 'does NOT do' section. More readable than checklist-based formats.

raw_candidates.name: Platform-specific skill variants
raw_candidates.description: 7 platform-specific skill files with shared core and platform-specific adaptations (Agent vs Task tool, hook availability, sequential fallback).

raw_candidates.name: ARCHITECTURE.md single-page format
raw_candidates.description: Pipeline, module table, extraction schema, confidence labels, how-to-extend, security, testing — all on one page. No bloat.

raw_candidates.name: Graph-only persistence without versioning
raw_candidates.description: graph.json has no schema_version, no migration path. Format changes break old graphs silently. SoNash analysis.json schema_version is the right approach.

raw_candidates.name: Oversized skill files (15K+ tokens per variant)
raw_candidates.description: 7 skill files with significant content duplication. 15K+ tokens each. LLM context waste and maintenance burden.

raw_candidates.name: Ship fast skip review pattern
raw_candidates.description: 20 releases in 6 days with zero merged external PRs. Security mitigations are self-assessed only. Impressive velocity but fragile.

safishamsi-graphify.relevance_to_home: Confidence tagging is a near-immediate upgrade for SoNash's analysis findings and /recall — the CAS currently has no provenance label per claim. The MCP stateless + token-budget pattern is a lighter alternative for any JASON-OS MCP work, and the 10-step skill orchestration gives a concrete reference for multi-step skill design.

raw_themes.name: Meta-memory: skills about HOW to remember
raw_themes.short_desc: Skills aren't memories; they're instructions for what kinds of memory to extract, how to remember, what to preserve or forget. Paradigm shift for auto-memory.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Skills are NOT experiential/procedural memory. Rather, they are a form of meta-memory that focuses on what kinds of memory to extract, how to remember and where to focus, and what to preserve or forget.

raw_themes.name: Skill evolution loop (failure-mined refinement)
raw_themes.short_desc: Mine memory failures, analyze, refine skills, propose new ones, repeat. Self-improving. Applies beyond memory (skill-audit, pattern-compliance).
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The skill evolution loop (mine failures, analyze, refine skills, propose new ones, repeat) is the mechanism. Skills start from data, not from human design.

raw_themes.name: 15 memory skill templates (5-section format)
raw_themes.short_desc: Description/Purpose/When to Use/How to Apply/Constraints format. Near-interchangeable with SoNash SKILL.md but about memory ops instead of workflows.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Each is a 5-section Markdown (Description/Purpose/When to Use/How to Apply/Constraints) with Action type.

raw_themes.name: Dual-embedding memory bank
raw_themes.short_desc: Content + context embeddings per memory item. Concept portable, code tied to FAISS+PyTorch.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Dual-Embedding Memory Bank... Content + context embeddings per memory item.

raw_themes.name: Academic code quality, strong ideas
raw_themes.short_desc: 42KB main.py monolith, zero tests, 18 unpinned deps, sys.path.append imports. Extract concepts, never depend.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 393 stars, 1 contributor, no tests, no CI, no releases. Research artifact, not maintained library. The 42KB main.py monolith signals academic code quality.

raw_candidates.name: Meta-Memory Skills Framework
raw_candidates.description: Skills about HOW to remember. Core concept for JASON-OS memory. Paradigm shift for auto-memory design.

raw_candidates.name: Skill Evolution Loop
raw_candidates.description: Mine failures, analyze, refine skills, propose new ones. Self-improving. General-purpose for any skill system (skill-audit, pattern-compliance).

raw_candidates.name: Skill Bank 5-Section Format
raw_candidates.description: Description/Purpose/When to Use/How to Apply/Constraints. Comparison template for SoNash SKILL.md.

raw_candidates.name: Designer Prompt Templates
raw_candidates.description: 18KB failure classification + mutation prompts. Methodology for skill refinement.

raw_candidates.name: Dual-Embedding Memory Bank
raw_candidates.description: Content + context embeddings per memory item. Concept portable, code tied to FAISS+PyTorch.

raw_candidates.name: Operation Templates with Meta-Info
raw_candidates.description: Usage + reward + EMA tracking for skill selection. Tool/skill selection system.

raw_candidates.name: 15 Memory Skill Templates
raw_candidates.description: Direct templates for JASON-OS memory operations. 8 conversational + 7 embodied skills.

raw_candidates.name: capture_activity_details.md
raw_candidates.description: Template for capturing activity details with temporal context. Applicable to auto-memory.

raw_candidates.name: insert.md
raw_candidates.description: Memory insert with duplicate avoidance, fact splitting, quality criteria.

raw_candidates.name: arXiv 2602.02474 (NOT FETCHED)
raw_candidates.description: Core theory: dual-embedding memory bank, failure mining, skill mutation strategies. MUST read before T4 execution.

raw_candidates.name: Academic code quality in production
raw_candidates.description: 42KB monolith, zero tests, no version pins, unsafe deserialization. Extract concepts, not code.

raw_candidates.name: Research artifact as dependency
raw_candidates.description: Paper companion code won't be maintained. Extract knowledge, don't depend.

viktoraxelsen-memskill.relevance_to_home: The most directly JASON-OS-relevant source in the slice for memory/auto-memory: meta-memory framing challenges SoNash's static auto-memory rules, and the 15 skill templates plus arXiv 2602.02474 are the foundation for JASON-OS memory research (T4). Must read the paper before T4 execution.

raw_themes.name: Ingest vs absorb (mechanical vs intellectual)
raw_themes.short_desc: Ingest = read/extract/write entries. Absorb = understand meaning and update the web of knowledge. SoNash repo-analysis ingests; synthesis begins to absorb; nothing flows back into living artifacts.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: the difference between ingesting knowledge and absorbing it. Ingest is mechanical (read source, extract facts, write entries). Absorb is intellectual (read entry, understand meaning, update the web of understanding).

raw_themes.name: Anti-cramming / anti-thinning balance
raw_themes.short_desc: Cramming = everything into same 5 big articles. Thinning = many stubs without depth. EXTRACTIONS.md has 81 candidates flat; some sources 17, some 5.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: **Cramming**: everything goes into the same 5 big articles (your equivalent: SESSION_CONTEXT.md absorbing everything) - **Thinning**: creating many stubs without depth (your equivalent: MEMORY.md entries that are one-liners)

raw_themes.name: 15-entry checkpoint cycle during batch ops
raw_themes.short_desc: Every N items during long batch operations, pause to rebuild indices, audit quality, check anti-patterns. Mid-pipeline quality gate.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 15-entry checkpoint cycle — during batch operations (like running `/repo-synthesis` across 6 repos), pause every N items to rebuild indices, audit quality, and check for anti-patterns.

raw_themes.name: Writer-not-filing-clerk identity
raw_themes.short_desc: LLM's job is to understand and synthesize, not categorize and file. Extraction journal is pure filing.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: **Writer-not-filing-clerk identity** — the framing that the LLM's job is to understand and synthesize, not categorize and file.

raw_themes.name: 7-command wiki skill architecture
raw_themes.short_desc: ingest/absorb/query/cleanup/breakdown/rebuild-index/reorganize. Concrete instantiation of Karpathy's ingest-query-lint triad with more granularity. T24 reference.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The 7-command architecture maps cleanly to your existing skills

raw_themes.name: Diary-driven structure anti-pattern
raw_themes.short_desc: Organizing by date instead of theme — SESSION_HISTORY.md is pure chronology. Steve Jobs test: 'Early life/Career (by era)' beats event-specific headings.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: **Diary structure**: organizing by date instead of by theme (your equivalent: SESSION_HISTORY.md is pure chronology)

raw_themes.name: No session continuity (gist-scope gap)
raw_themes.short_desc: farzaa's skill assumes one long session. No compaction, no checkpoint. SoNash infrastructure (SESSION_CONTEXT.md, /checkpoint) is an entire layer he lacks.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: **He has no session continuity.** His skill assumes one long session. No compaction handling, no checkpoint/restore, no cross-session state beyond `_absorb_log.json`.

raw_candidates.name: Anti-cramming / anti-thinning balance for knowledge skills
raw_candidates.description: When to split vs enrich. If adding third paragraph about sub-topic, split. If stub exists with 4 entries mentioning it, enrich. Directly applicable to /repo-synthesis and /website-synthesis granularity.

raw_candidates.name: 15-entry checkpoint cycle pattern
raw_candidates.description: Periodic maintenance rhythm during long operations. Every N items: rebuild indexes, audit quality, check for anti-patterns. Mid-session companion to session-end.

raw_candidates.name: Parallel subagent cleanup workflow (5-batch audit)
raw_candidates.description: Spawn batches of 5 agents, each audits one article across 6 dimensions (structure, length, tone, quotes, coherence, links). Deduplicate, then fix. Comparable to code-reviewer + skill-audit.

raw_candidates.name: Writer-not-filing-clerk identity framing
raw_candidates.description: Identity statement shapes every operation: 'what does this mean and how does it connect' vs 'where do I file this'. Reframes analysis/synthesis skill output.

raw_candidates.name: Concurrency safety rules for LLM file ops
raw_candidates.description: Re-read before editing, never delete without reading, rebuild indices only at command end. Partially captured in CLAUDE.md guardrails.

raw_candidates.name: 7-command skill architecture as T24 reference design
raw_candidates.description: ingest/absorb/query/cleanup/breakdown/rebuild-index/reorganize — concrete instantiation of Karpathy's ingest-query-lint triad with more granularity. Direct reference for T24 synthesis adoption skill design.

farzaa-gist-c35ac0cf.relevance_to_home: The most directly T24-relevant source: farzaa's 7-command architecture names the exact gap in SoNash CAS — 'absorb' (weave analysis outputs into living knowledge) and 'breakdown' (mine knowledge for missing entities) are both missing. Also surfaces anti-cramming/anti-thinning critique that applies to EXTRACTIONS.md.

raw_themes.name: Breadth-coverage calibration document
raw_themes.short_desc: Public tutorial covering every major Claude Code feature — useful not for learning but for checking SoNash's feature coverage against an external expert's checklist.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The value isn't learning — it's calibration.

raw_themes.name: Junior engineer mental model
raw_themes.short_desc: Treat Claude Code as mid-level engineer, not autopilot. External validation of SoNash guardrails (#1-15): plan-before-implement, stop-on-correction, never-push-without-approval.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The article frames Claude Code as a "mid-level software engineer" and recommends treating it that way. Our CLAUDE.md guardrails (#1-15) encode a stricter version of this

raw_themes.name: Cloud teleportation pattern (& prefix)
raw_themes.short_desc: Send long-running tasks to Claude Code Web with & prefix, pull results locally. Potential solution for terminal-blocking background agents.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Cloud teleportation pattern (& prefix) ... Send long-running tasks to Claude Code Web with & prefix, pull results locally.

raw_themes.name: Plugin marketplace watch
raw_themes.short_desc: Anthropic marketplace with 36 curated plugins (Dec 2025). Not yet adopted by SoNash — skills cover the ground; worth tracking ecosystem maturity.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Mentions the Anthropic marketplace (36 plugins, Dec 2025). We haven't adopted plugins — our skills and hooks cover the same ground.

raw_themes.name: Features-without-enforcement anti-pattern
raw_themes.short_desc: Tutorial describes hooks as automation without describing them as gates. CLAUDE.md as memory without rules. No concept of guardrails that override judgment.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Hooks are described as "automation" but not as "gates." CLAUDE.md is described as "memory" but not as "non-negotiable rules." There's no concept of guardrails that override Claude's judgment.

raw_themes.name: Meta-tooling layer absent from public discourse
raw_themes.short_desc: No mention of tools that audit your tools — skill audits, hook ecosystem audits, pattern compliance. The article treats Claude Code as product to configure, not platform to build on.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: No mention of tools that audit your tools — skill audits, hook ecosystem audits, pattern compliance checking, learning effectiveness analysis. The article treats Claude Code as a product to configure, not a platform to build on.

raw_themes.name: Compaction problem unaddressed
raw_themes.short_desc: Article mentions /compact but never discusses state loss, checkpoint strategies, or compaction-resilient artifact design. Major operational blind spot.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Brief mention of `/compact` but no discussion of state loss during compaction, checkpoint strategies, or how to design artifacts that survive context resets.

raw_candidates.name: Cloud teleportation pattern (& prefix)
raw_candidates.description: Send long-running tasks to Claude Code Web with & prefix, pull results locally with --teleport. Could solve terminal-blocking agent problem.

raw_candidates.name: Plugin marketplace ecosystem status
raw_candidates.description: Anthropic marketplace (36 curated plugins, Dec 2025). Community marketplaces emerging. Not yet adopted by SoNash — worth tracking maturity.

raw_candidates.name: Junior engineer framing validation
raw_candidates.description: External validation that treating Claude Code as a junior engineer (not autopilot) produces best results. Aligns with SoNash guardrails approach.

raw_candidates.name: Tutorial structure as onboarding template
raw_candidates.description: Setup -> Daily Use -> Advanced -> Production progression. 20 sections with clear hierarchy. Could template a SoNash onboarding guide.

raw_candidates.name: Feature-breadth coverage as completeness benchmark
raw_candidates.description: Checklist of Claude Code features an external expert considers important. Use to verify CLAUDE.md and skill coverage.

raw_candidates.name: Features without enforcement anti-pattern
raw_candidates.description: Describing hooks as automation without gates. CLAUDE.md as memory without rules. Misses the reliability layer entirely.

raw_candidates.name: No compaction discussion anti-pattern
raw_candidates.description: Treating context as unlimited. No state loss discussion, no checkpoint strategies. Major operational gap for power users.

sidbharath-com-blog-claude-code-the-comp.relevance_to_home: Calibration source rather than extraction source: confirms SoNash is well ahead on enforcement, meta-tooling, and compaction-resilience. Cloud teleportation and plugin marketplace are the only two ideas worth tracking. The article's blind spots (no enforcement, no meta-tooling, no compaction) map exactly onto SoNash's strongest moats.

initial_clusters.cluster_name: MCP / CLI-wrapping / agent-native tool exposure (JASON-OS Domain 02a)
initial_clusters.short_desc: Four sources converge on patterns for exposing software/tools/data to AI agents via MCP or CLI wrappers. ArchiveBox auto-derives MCP schemas from CLI; CLI-Anything codifies the wrapping methodology; graphify shows a minimal stateless MCP server. Directly feeds JASON-OS Domain 02a.

initial_clusters.cluster_name: Skill/hook architecture — format, orchestration, and governance

member_themes.theme_name: Hook execution model with runtime semantics

initial_clusters.short_desc: Six sources offer competing/complementary SKILL.md and skill-orchestration designs: command catalogs vs workflow definitions, 5-section memory templates, 7-command wiki, 10-step pipelines, runtime hook semantics. SoNash sits in the middle — its workflow-definition SKILL.md is sophisticated but lacks runtime hook semantics, retirement workflows, and absorb/breakdown commands.

initial_clusters.cluster_name: Ingest vs absorb / confidence vs flat extraction

member_themes.theme_name: Meta-memory: skills about HOW to remember

initial_clusters.short_desc: Three sources converge on the theme that knowledge extraction needs more than flat candidate lists — it needs provenance (graphify confidence tags), absorption back into living artifacts (farzaa), and meta-rules about what/how to remember (MemSkill). Current SoNash CAS produces flat extraction candidates with no provenance and no absorb step.

member_themes.theme_name: Missing retirement process as catalog failure mode
Governance gap behind healthy community signal

initial_clusters.short_desc: Four different popular repos (486K / 58K / 28K / 27K stars) all exhibit variants of the same failure mode: community velocity and star counts outpace test/review/retirement infrastructure. Validates SoNash's heavy pre-commit/PR-review/skill-audit investment — and the need for a skill-retirement workflow before the index grows further.

member_themes.theme_name: Governance gap behind healthy community signal
Adversarial-input security posture for AI agents

initial_clusters.short_desc: Five sources contrast security posture: ArchiveBox and MinerU ship with permissive defaults (no auth, no rate limit); CLI-Anything names the adversarial-agent threat explicitly; graphify organizes docs by attack vector; Sid's tutorial misses enforcement layer entirely. SoNash's security-auditor / App Check / sanitizeError stack is the correct counter-model.

initial_clusters.cluster_name: Documentation as product surface (schema, env-vars, decision matrices)

member_themes.theme_name: Environment variable catalog as single document

initial_clusters.short_desc: Three sources treat documentation as a product surface rather than an afterthought: MinerU's output_files.md (schema-as-contract), env-var catalog, and backend decision matrix; graphify's single-page ARCHITECTURE.md; ArchiveBox's 498-line CLAUDE.md. Applies to SoNash CAS: ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md and ENV_VARS.md are half-day writes that close real drift gaps.

absence_signals.expected_because: SoNash is a Sober Nashville recovery notebook; the only place agents could find recovery-specific UX patterns would be dedicated product sources.
absence_signals.suggestion: Add sources focused on recovery/sobriety/journaling/mental-health UX (e.g., recovery.com, i-am-sober app design posts, AA literature structure). Current corpus treats SoNash purely as an engineering project.

absence_signals.expected_because: SoNash is Next.js 16 + Firebase 12, with strict 'no direct writes via httpsCallable' contract. Zero analyzed sources in this slice cover Firebase architecture patterns, Firestore security rules, App Check, or Cloud Functions cold-start mitigation.
absence_signals.suggestion: Add sources on Firebase production architecture (Firebase summits, Google Cloud Next talks, firebase-admin patterns) so SoNash's security/patterns §5 has external validation.

absence_signals.expected_because: JASON-OS targets a portable Claude Code OS. MCP patterns in this slice are all Python (ArchiveBox, CLI-Anything, graphify). Build-vs-integrate decision hinges on finding a Node/TS reference.
absence_signals.suggestion: Analyze @modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk reference servers, or a TS-based MCP server like FastMCP-TS or the Anthropic MCP example repo in TypeScript.

absence_signals.expected_because: codecrafters/build-your-own-x exposed the failure mode (no retirement) and hkuds/cli-anything shows the same risk at higher velocity. But no source in the slice demonstrates a working retirement/deprecation workflow.
absence_signals.suggestion: Analyze projects with mature deprecation processes (Rails deprecation_warning, Python PEP 387, Node.js deprecation cycle docs, npm deprecate command, a skill-registry project with real retirement in its changelog).

absence_signals.expected_because: MinerU's async task state machine is in-memory only (loses state on restart). T28 Gap Agent A Q5 asks for progressive extraction. The adoption-ready reference (crawl4ai with resume_state) sits outside this slice.
absence_signals.suggestion: Ensure crawl4ai (in another slice) feeds the same cluster, and add a persistence-layer source — e.g., Temporal.io workflow engine, durable-execution pattern docs, or EventStore architecture — for the 'survive restart' shape.

notes: Heavy cross-pollination within this slice: cross_repo_connections reference each other (archivebox <-> cli-anything, cli-anything <-> memskill, graphify <-> memskill). Parent synthesis should dedupe candidate names that appear in multiple sources' value-maps (e.g., skill-format patterns).

MinerU creator-view.md exceeded the 10K token read limit; full value-map.json was read and the first 200 lines of creator-view were read — themes/candidates were derived from those plus the summary in value-map. No information from the unread tail was asserted.

All sources in this slice are classified 'defer' in the extraction journal — per HOME_CONTEXT, synthesis is the pass that picks what to act on first. Top T1 candidates for act-first ranking from this slice: MCP auto-discovery (archivebox), HARNESS.md methodology (cli-anything), output_files.md + env-vars (MinerU), confidence tagging (graphify), meta-memory skills (memskill), 7-command wiki architecture (farzaa).

Several candidates are Python-bound (CLI-Anything harnesses, graphify's Python MCP server, MemSkill's FAISS+PyTorch). The build-vs-integrate decision for JASON-OS will determine whether the LIFT is code or methodology.

farzaa gist and Sid Bharath blog are the two 'mirror' sources that validate SoNash's existing posture without adding net-new candidates — except farzaa's absorb/breakdown commands, which name a real gap in the CAS pipeline.

Consistent anti-pattern across 4 of 6 repo sources: growth/popularity outpaced quality enforcement. This convergence is strong enough that parent synthesis should treat it as a single cross-corpus anti-pattern cluster rather than 4 separate items.

Three sources independently surface 'schema versioning / output contract' patterns (MinerU output_files.md, graphify graph-only-no-versioning anti-pattern, SoNash's own analysis.json schema_version). Parent should make this a first-class synthesis theme.
